product_overview: A FastAPI backend that generates AI-driven "skill" packages from creator content via two workflows (v1 raw-paste and v2 with Twitter/YouTube ingestion), and provides listing, export, and management endpoints.
core_goals: Provide a reliable API to generate skill packages from pasted content, Twitter timelines, or YouTube transcripts.
Expose listing, detail, export (zip and individual files), and delete operations for generated skills.
Validate inputs and block path traversal attempts to protect the filesystem.
Surface clear error codes for validation, external API failures, and internal generation issues.
Keep a simple, discoverable API surface (root service info and health checks) and maintain docs at /docs.

features.name: Health and Info
features.description: Root service info and a health-check endpoint for service discovery and basic uptime monitoring.
user_flows: GET / -> Receive 200 ServiceInfo with endpoint map and workflow descriptions -> GET /api/health -> Receive 200 {"status":"ok"}
GET /api/health -> Receive 500 Internal Server Error (service unhealthy)

features.name: V1 Skill Generation
features.description: Generate a skill package from pasted creator content (minimum 40 characters); returns metadata and created asset locations.
user_flows: POST /api/v1/generate-skill with body {creator_content: '... >= 40 chars ...', desired_skill_name: 'MySkill', persist_to_disk: true, include_zip: true} -> Receive 200 GenerateSkillResponse with skill_name and zip path -> GET /api/v1/skills/{skill_name} -> Receive 200 SkillDetail with files and contents
POST /api/v1/generate-skill with body {creator_content: 'short'} -> Receive 422 Pydantic validation error (string_too_short)
POST /api/v1/generate-skill with valid body -> Receive 500 Internal server error (CrewAI pipeline failure or timeout)

features.name: V2 Twitter Integration
features.description: Ingest the last 25 tweets (no retweets) from a Twitter/X user, transform them into creator content, and generate a skill with source metadata.
user_flows: POST /api/v2/generate-skill/twitter with body {twitter_username: 'creator123', desired_skill_name: 'TwitterSkill', target_outcome: 'summarize'} and X_BEARER_TOKEN configured -> Receive 200 GenerateSkillResponse with skill_name and source_metadata (tweet ids, counts) -> GET /api/v2/skills/{skill_name} -> Receive 200 SkillDetail with generated files and source_metadata
POST /api/v2/generate-skill/twitter with body {twitter_username: 'creator123'} and X_BEARER_TOKEN not configured -> Receive 400 'X_BEARER_TOKEN not configured' or 'no tweets found'
POST /api/v2/generate-skill/twitter with body {twitter_username: 'nonexistent'} and X API returns error -> Receive 502 External X API error (auth, not found, rate limit)

features.name: V2 YouTube Integration
features.description: Fetch transcripts from one or more YouTube video URLs, consolidate transcripts into creator content, and generate a skill with source metadata and warnings when transcripts are partial/missing.
user_flows: POST /api/v2/generate-skill/youtube with body {youtube_urls: ['https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=abc123'], desired_skill_name: 'YTSkill'} -> Receive 200 GenerateSkillResponse with skill_name, source_metadata and potential warnings -> GET /api/v2/skills/{skill_name} -> Receive 200 SkillDetail including transcript-derived files
POST /api/v2/generate-skill/youtube with body {youtube_urls: []} -> Receive 422 Pydantic validation error (min items)
POST /api/v2/generate-skill/youtube with invalid or non-transcriptable URLs -> Receive 400 YouTubeError (no valid URLs or no transcripts)

features.name: Skill Listing and Detail
features.description: List all generated skills with file summaries or fetch a single skill's full detail including file contents; allow deletion of skill directories and zips.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/skills -> Receive 200 SkillList with count and file summaries -> GET /api/v1/skills/{skill_name} -> Receive 200 SkillDetail with files and content
GET /api/v1/skills/{skill_name} for nonexistent skill -> Receive 404 Skill not found
DELETE /api/v1/skills/{skill_name} -> Receive 200 Deletion confirmation -> GET /api/v1/skills/{skill_name} -> Receive 404 Skill not found
GET /api/v2/skills -> Receive 200 SkillList with count (v1 and v2 share same storage directory)

features.name: Export and Download
features.description: Download a full skill package as a zip, fetch single files (text/markdown), or request raw file content for clipboard copy.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/export/{skill_name}/zip -> Receive 200 ZIP file (application/zip) -> GET /api/v1/export/{skill_name}/{file_path} -> Receive 200 File content (text/markdown or text/plain) -> POST /api/v1/export/copy with body {skill_name: 'MySkill', file_path: 'SKILL.md'} -> Receive 200 JSON with content and file_path
GET /api/v1/export/{missing_skill}/zip -> Receive 404 Zip not found
GET /api/v1/export/{skill_name}/{file_path_with_..} -> Receive 400 Invalid path (path traversal attempt blocked)

features.name: Security Middleware (Path Traversal Guard)
features.description: Middleware detects and blocks requests that attempt path traversal ("..") in exported file paths, returning 400 Invalid path.
user_flows: GET /api/v1/export/{skill_name}/{safe_file_path} -> Receive 200 File content
GET /api/v1/export/..%2F..%2Fetc/passwd -> Receive 400 Invalid path (path traversal detected and blocked by middleware)

known_limitations.issue: CrewAI generation takes 60-120 seconds per call
known_limitations.impact: Requests to generate-skill endpoints may timeout on short HTTP timeouts

known_limitations.issue: Generated skills are stored on ephemeral filesystem
known_limitations.impact: Skills are lost on Railway redeploy

known_limitations.issue: V1 and V2 share the same generated_skills directory
known_limitations.impact: Skills from both workflows appear in both /api/v1/skills and /api/v2/skills
